debutant-4-semaines.title: Beginner — 4 weeks
debutant-4-semaines.description: A progressive program to ease into working out
debutant-4-semaines.headline: Build your foundations
debutant-4-semaines.intro: Getting back into sport or starting out? This 4-week program guides you step by step. Each week adds a little more intensity so you progress without ever feeling overwhelmed.
debutant-4-semaines.audience: Returning to activity, true beginners, or anyone who wants to (re)start on solid ground.
debutant-4-semaines.approach: The progression is designed so you don't skip steps. We start with simple movements and generous rest, then shorten the breaks and add fuller variations as the weeks go by.

weeks.title: Discovery
weeks.description: Basic movements, 2 sets, generous rest. Learning the fundamental moves: squats, adapted push-ups, planks.

weeks.title: Building habits
weeks.description: Same improved patterns, 2-3 sets, reps increasing. Your body starts to memorize the movements.

weeks.title: Progression
weeks.description: Full variations (feet-elevated plank, standard push-ups), 3 sets. Introducing the EMOM format to learn effort management.

weeks.title: Consolidation
weeks.description: Combined circuits, supersets, increased volume. You've mastered the basics and are ready for what's next.

benefits: Prepare your body for more intense programs
debutant-4-semaines.tip: No need to push hard from day one. Consistency is what matters most. 3 sessions per week is enough to see real results in a month.

remise-en-forme.title: Get back in shape
remise-en-forme.description: Comprehensive cardio and strength training program
remise-en-forme.headline: Find your rhythm back
remise-en-forme.intro: You used to train but fell off the wagon? This program gets things back on track. Varied formats from the first week, progressive intensity — you'll find your feel quickly.
remise-en-forme.audience: Former athletes getting back in shape, or intermediate-level people who want to structure their training.
remise-en-forme.approach: No discovery phase here: we dive straight in with varied formats. Each week alternates upper body, lower body, full-body, and cardio for a complete, balanced workout.

weeks.title: Active restart
weeks.description: Targeted strength work and first circuits. We wake up the muscle groups with structured sessions.

weeks.title: Loading up
weeks.description: Volume and intensity increase. Supersets and EMOM to build muscular endurance.

weeks.title: Intensity peak
weeks.description: Demanding formats: AMRAP, HIIT. Your body is ready to be pushed. Sessions are shorter but more intense.

weeks.title: Mastery
weeks.description: Advanced combinations, complex circuits. You finish the program in top shape, ready for a new challenge.

benefits: Quickly regain a good level of fitness
Work your whole body in a balanced way
remise-en-forme.tip: If a session feels easy, that's normal — it means you're in shape for it. The following week will raise the bar.

cardio-express.title: Cardio Express
cardio-express.description: Short and intense HIIT and Tabata sessions
cardio-express.headline: Short, intense, effective
cardio-express.intro: No time for an hour of sport? This program bets on short, high-intensity sessions. 20 minutes is all it takes to sweat, progress, and get back to your day.
cardio-express.audience: Busy schedules, athletes who want results without spending hours training.
cardio-express.approach: Every session uses formats designed to maximize effort in minimal time: HIIT, Tabata, EMOM. Intensity builds progressively so your cardio and explosiveness improve week after week.

weeks.title: Warm-up phase
weeks.description: HIIT and classic intervals. We calibrate the intensity and establish the rhythm.

weeks.title: Acceleration
weeks.description: Tabata and EMOM come into play. Rest periods shorten, cardio kicks up a notch.

weeks.title: High intensity
weeks.description: Denser sessions, explosive combinations. Your heart is working at full capacity.

weeks.title: Full throttle
weeks.description: The most intense sessions of the program. You measure your progress with timed formats.

benefits: Improve your cardio in less time
Train even with a packed schedule
cardio-express.tip: Intensity is the key. 20 minutes at full effort beats 45 minutes at half pace. Give everything during the work phases, and fully recover during the rest.
